github_actions_trigger_exact_content.on_block:
on:
  schedule:
    - cron: "*/30 * * * *"
  workflow_dispatch: {} # allows a manual "Run workflow" click for testing
github_actions_trigger_exact_content.evidence: user-provided screenshot of run #6, 'Triggered via schedule', on main, Success -- contradicts the file's own header comment claiming it was 'prepared but not yet enabled end-to-end'

live_flags_production.verification_method: vercel env pull --environment=production, literal values read (no secrets displayed -- these are plain feature-flag booleans, not credentials)

proof_no_active_invocation.reasoning: The only mechanism that would represent an 'active' invocation is this exact lock sentinel row (run-lock.ts's compare-and-set design has no separate heartbeat/liveness table). It was created 2026-07-18T17:46:28 by the run that failed with FUNCTION_INVOCATION_TIMEOUT; all three write-authorizing flags have been false since shortly after that failure (confirmed above), so no new invocation could have started since, and none has (no second lock row exists, no rotation/budget row shows a last_run_id newer than the seed).
